## Acceptance criteria

- `GET /api/raw/*path` is wired in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:31-46`, and `get_raw_wildcard()` passes `?commit=` through to `CommandExecutor::read_path_bytes()` in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:654-691` and `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:563-569`. The storage implementation resolves pinned blobs with `revparse_single()`/`tree.get_path()` in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:781-809`.
- Path-traversal and absolute-path rejection are centralized at the storage boundary in `validate_repo_relative_input()` (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:852-885`), with coverage for `../etc/passwd`, `a/../../b`, and the allowed `foo..bar.txt`/`archive..2024.md` cases in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1882-1924`.
- Symlink escapes are blocked uniformly by canonicalization plus repo-root containment in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:811-847`, and covered both at storage level (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1926-1977`) and across all three raw handlers (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:2390-2558`).
- HTML base-tag injection is implemented once via `compute_base_href()`, `html_has_base_element()`, and `inject_base_href()` in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:370-491`, then applied only when `Content-Type` is `text/html` in all raw handlers at `crates/server/src/routes.rs:567-573`, `620-626`, and `673-679`.
- Existing `<base>` tags are preserved, comment/script false positives are avoided, and the helper coverage includes `<head>`, `<head ...attrs...>`, no `<head>`, empty input, existing `<base>`, and DOCTYPE/comment-only HTML in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:2016-2119`.
- Non-HTML byte-faithfulness is covered for issue-scoped binary reads in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:1563-1624` and wildcard SVG reads in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:2183-2201`.
- I could not independently inspect a matching PR description for the manual gf2 deck verification, but I found no remaining code-side gap in the implementation for that scenario.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

- The earlier route-local filesystem checks have been pushed into storage. `read_document_bytes()` now forwards the stored repo-relative path instead of pre-resolving filesystem paths in `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:381-392`.
- Repo containment and path validation now live in `JsonFileStorage::read_path_bytes()` (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:768-885`), while the server layer stays focused on HTTP concerns in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:547-691`.
- That boundary is clean and removes the technical debt called out in earlier failed reviews.

## Functional paradigm

- The new logic is mostly small pure helpers (`compute_base_href`, `html_has_base_element`, `inject_base_href`) in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:370-491`, with side effects confined to the storage boundary in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:761-849`.
- I did not find a blocking regression toward imperative or cross-layer logic.

## Correctness and invariants

- Repo-root containment is enforced uniformly before working-tree reads via canonicalization and `starts_with()` in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:811-847`.
- Invalid repo-relative inputs are rejected before any read in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:852-885`.
- `PathReadError::InvalidPath` and `PathReadError::OutsideRepoRoot` map to HTTP 400 in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:273-280`, which is the correct contract for traversal/symlink rejection.
- The touched code does not affect event logging, gate semantics, assignee/label parsing, or PID handling.

## Safety

- I found no new `unsafe` usage in the touched paths, and nothing here weakens the workspace’s `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` posture.
- No PID-sensitive code was changed.

## Testing

- The required validation path passed: `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` reported success with label warnings only at `/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777069411602-4b8hye.txt:12-14`.
- `cargo test --workspace` passed cleanly, including the full workspace run at `/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777069411602-4b8hye.txt:801` and the later crate/doc-test summaries through `/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777069411602-4b8hye.txt:2818-2824`.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` completed cleanly at `/tmp/copilot-tool-output-1777069411602-4b8hye.txt:2826-2828`.
- Issue-specific coverage is now complete for the earlier missing `foo..bar` case in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1891-1924`.

## Error handling

- Path read failures use typed `PathReadError` variants with contextual messages in `crates/jit/src/storage/path_errors.rs:21-42`.
- Route handlers branch on those typed errors instead of string matching in `crates/server/src/routes.rs:273-280`, `554-560`, `607-613`, and `661-667`.
- I did not find new `unwrap()`/`expect()` misuse in non-test library code related to this change.

## CLI contract

- No CLI surface changed in this work, so there is no regression against the `--json` requirement.

## Documentation

- The touched public APIs retain doc comments with examples: `CommandExecutor::read_document_bytes()` in `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:340-365`, `CommandExecutor::read_path_bytes()` in `crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:548-569`, and `PathReadError` in `crates/jit/src/storage/path_errors.rs:5-42`.
- I did not find a public-API documentation regression.

## Prior review feedback

- The prior architecture/security complaint is resolved by moving path validation and repo containment into `JsonFileStorage::read_path_bytes()` (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:768-885`) and simplifying `read_document_bytes()` (`crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:381-392`).
- The last remaining failed-review item, missing positive coverage for filenames containing `..`, is now closed by `test_read_path_bytes_allows_dots_inside_segment()` in `crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:1891-1924`.
- I did not find any unresolved blocker from the latest `run_history` entry.

## JIT issue management

- `issue.dependencies` is empty in the provided issue context, so there is no prerequisite work to verify.
- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed, and its warnings are label warnings, which are explicitly allowed.
- The issue metadata already shows `cargo-ci` and `npm-ci` as passed; this review does not uncover a reason to keep `code-review` failed.
